commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Bump version to 0.3.4 — restart now kills and waits for process death

Restart commands (paint restart, paint bridge restart, paint terminal restart)
now use killAndWait() which sends SIGTERM, waits up to 5s, then escalates to
SIGKILL if the process is still alive. This ensures the port is released before
starting the new process.

commit.message:
fix(merge): update ALL bounds after ring merge, not just first (#53)

Remove `break` statements in merge update loops that prevented updating
all bounds with the removed ring index. In Rust (unlike C++ with pointer
equality), multiple bounds can share the same ring index and all need
to be updated after a merge.

Fixes issue #53 partially:
- Golden tests: 56 passing (up from 39) - 17 more tests pass
- Unit test added to verify correct behavior

Deeper issue remains: after merge participants are set to `ring=None`,
subsequent intersections with different polygon types can still create
spurious rings. This needs further investigation.

Files changed:
- intersect_util.rs: Remove break in process_intersect_list merge handler
- ring_util.rs: Remove break in append_ring bound update loop
- process_horizontal.rs: Remove break in horizontal merge handlers (2 places)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(video): revision routing centralization and test import (014 follow-up) (#35)

* spec(014): multi-agent video production architecture

Define 6-agent video planning system with:
- Story Writer: narrative arc, visual anchor
- Copywriter: hooks, captions, CTA
- Stitch Planner: production method selection (NEW)
- Prompt Engineer: service-specific prompts
- Reviewer: quality gate
- Orchestrator: flow control

Key design decisions:
- Agent order: stitch_planner before prompt_engineer (generation method determines prompt type)
- Marketing layer extension points: campaign_context, performance_hints, experiment_vars
- StitchPlan/AssetManifest contracts defined
- VideoPlan extension strategy: Option A (extend model)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* spec(014): add implementation details for P0-P3

- P0-1: Stitch Planner references SERVICE_CONSTRAINTS
- P0-2: Agent order with fan-out (copywriter || prompt_engineer)
- P0-3: Cascade invalidation logic
- P1-1: Reviewer integrates with VideoPlanScorer
- P1-2: ShotDraft → VideoShot mapping with notes preservation
- P1-3: Error handling with safe_node wrapper
- P2-1: Cost/token tracking (llm_calls, token_budget)
- P2-2: Prompt file loading via extended prompt_loader
- P2-3: Account config with visual_settings and channels
- P3: LangGraph dependency (uv add langgraph)

Updated VideoAgentState with new fields and added v3 change history.

* plan(014): multi-agent video implementation plan

5-Phase implementation plan for multi-agent video production system:
- Phase 1: Foundation (state, schemas, graph structure)
- Phase 2: Core Production Flow (story_writer, stitch_planner, prompt_engineer)
- Phase 3: Copy & Review Loop (copywriter, reviewer, orchestrator)
- Phase 4: Integration (VideoGenerator, VideoPlan extension)
- Phase 5: Testing & Polish

Key components:
- VideoAgentState TypedDict with marketing extension points
- ShotDraft, StitchPlan, AssetItem schemas
- Cascade invalidation in orchestrator
- Fan-out parallel execution (copywriter || prompt_engineer)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(014): address plan review issues

Critical fixes:
- Add narrative_transition to ShotDraft (story-to-stitch connection)
- Add reviewer → orchestrator edge (graph was broken)
- Fix route_by_revision for budget_exceeded and max_iterations
- Add prompt loader (prompts/loader.py)
- Add account_config initialization in VideoAgentGraph.generate()
- Document cascade re-execution tradeoff

Spec updates:
- narrative_transition field added to ShotDraft schema

Enterprise integration: standardizer, Nova rename, 8 expert reviews, GEO/SEO, budget validation

ARCHITECTURE:
- NEW standardizer.py: Canonical taxonomies (17 industries, 34 roles, 158 countries, 29 platforms)
- NEW monitoring.py: Structured logging, metrics, health checks, graceful shutdown
- RENAMED joveo_iq.py -> nova.py: Full rebrand, 18 tools, 12 data sources, conversation memory

DATA PIPELINE:
- KB loader expanded: 8 JSON files (850KB+) loaded into unified knowledge base
- All 25 API outputs now consumed (zero orphaned)
- 6 fuse functions + AI narratives in data_synthesizer.py
- Standardizer wired into api_enrichment, data_synthesizer, budget_engine, app.py

OUTPUT:
- PPT: 11 slides (5 new: Market Analysis, Location Analysis, Competitive Landscape, Workforce Trends, Budget Allocation)
- Excel: 4 new sheets (Campaign Projections, Competitive Landscape, Workforce Trends, Sources)
- Budget feasibility validation: 6-tier system (impossible -> generous), frontend warnings, Excel reality check
- Structured estimates: Channel-by-channel projections table, role distribution, feasibility assessment

SECURITY & RELIABILITY (SRE + Frontend + Backend reviews):
- ThreadedHTTPServer, ThreadPoolExecutor for Slack, rate limiter memory fix
- CSP headers, timing-safe auth, XSS fixes, input validation
- AbortController timeouts on all fetch calls, var->let fixes
- Disk cache file limits, structured logging (print->logger)

GEO/AEO/SEO:
- 4 JSON-LD blocks (SoftwareApplication, WebApplication, Organization, FAQPage)
- 16 meta tags (OG, Twitter Card, canonical, robots)
- robots.txt, sitemap.xml, static asset caching
- PPT/Excel document metadata for AI discoverability

NOVA CHATBOT:
- System prompt overhaul with 5-step reasoning, source citations, hallucination prevention
- 18 tools (added suggest_smart_defaults), confidence scoring v2
- Conversation memory system, max_iterations 5->8, timeout 30->45s

SLACK BOT:
- Per-thread conversation history (20 msg/thread, 1hr TTL)
- Slack channel history search for context
- Unanswered queue cap (500), learned answers cap (2000)
- Slack diagnostic endpoint (/api/slack/status)

commit.message:
fix(types): compile shared types to CJS and point exports to dist/

The @ayurnod/types package was exporting raw .ts source files and
compiling to ESNext (ESM). Backend compiles to CJS and runtime
require() calls for value imports (auth validation functions) failed
with 'Unexpected token export'. Now compiles to CommonJS and exports
resolve to dist/ for proper Node.js CJS compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add v1 public API, Pfam/clan search, shared query module, and security hardening

- Add /api/v1/ public API endpoints (domain, PDB, UniProt, health) with
  rate limiting (100 req/min) and CORS support via proxy.ts
- Add Pfam accession (PF#####) and clan (CL####) search types
- Extract shared domain-queries.ts module (getDomainDataPath, parseRange,
  classification JOINs) and refactor internal routes to use it
- Add /documentation/api page with interactive endpoint examples
- Security: add 30s statement_timeout, escapeLike() for ILIKE wildcards,
  suppress query params from production error logs
- Update default DB host from asteria to sangala

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: complete all 6 remaining marketing deliverables

Created comprehensive marketing strategy documents:

1. Onboarding Flow CRO (marketing/onboarding-flow-cro.md)
   - Identified critical issues (no dedicated flow, bank too early, no progress bar)
   - Redesigned 5-step onboarding: Welcome → Goal → Demo → Bank → Celebration
   - Expected impact: +60-100% activation rate improvement
   - A/B test variants and metrics tracking plan

2. Product Hunt Launch Strategy (marketing/product-hunt-launch-strategy.md)
   - Complete 4-week pre-launch checklist
   - Hour-by-hour launch day timeline
   - Product listing copy with pinned comment
   - Outreach templates and response scripts
   - Target: Top 5 product, 200+ upvotes, 100+ signups

3. Email Sequences (marketing/email-sequences.md)
   - 5-email welcome series (onboarding automation)
   - Re-engagement sequence (3 emails for inactive users)
   - Feature unlock celebrations
   - Goal milestone emails
   - Free→Paid upsell sequence
   - Target: 25-35% open rate, 15-20% activation

4. Social Media Calendar (marketing/social-media-30-day-calendar.md)
   - 30-day content calendar for Twitter/LinkedIn
   - 5 content pillars (product 40%, education 30%, BTS 15%, social proof 10%, engagement 5%)
   - Reusable post templates
   - Engagement strategy and response templates
   - Target: 10K+ impressions/month, 100+ link clicks

5. Competitor Comparisons (marketing/competitor-comparison-pages.md)
   - Steady vs YNAB (detailed comparison)
   - Steady vs Mint (migration guide)
   - Steady vs PocketSmith (NZ market)
   - Feature tables, pros/cons, user testimonials, FAQ
   - Target: 100-300 visitors/month per page (SEO)

6. Pricing Page Optimization (marketing/pricing-page-optimization.md)
   - 12 critical fixes (Most Popular badge, social proof, ROI calculator)
   - Optimized page structure (7 sections)
   - Feature→benefit copy improvements
   - 4 A/B test variants
   - Target: +20-40% Free→Paid conversion improvement

All documents are ready for implementation.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
ci: PRごとのVercel preview deployワークフロー追加 (#204)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch email delivery from SMTP to Resend HTTP API

Railway blocks outbound SMTP (port 587), causing all emails to fail
with "Network is unreachable". Replace smtplib with Resend's HTTP API
(port 443) in both waitlist welcome and password reset flows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add microcode generation with instruction set and fetch prefix

Implement generate_microcode() with 6-step fetch prefix, conditional
execution (8 condition codes from flags), ALU push/flags-only modes,
LDA immediate, and PUSH immediate. Add encode_instruction() helper,
_force_pc/_force_sp for testing, and 14 instruction-level tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Deploy God Templates Arsenal v1.0 — 10 elite AI command templates

god-templates/ (10 files):
- 01-Master-Task-List-God.md: Exhaustive phased task lists, 15-25 steps/phase, confluence checkpoints
- 02-Master-Brainstorm-God.md: Architecture-first coding — Phase 0 mandatory: 4+ approaches, scoring matrix, data flow map
- 03-ChenzO-Pipeline-God.md: Full 3-stage ChenzO pipeline with live data injection, self-upgrade log, resume capability
- 04-Weekly-Market-God.md: Sunday 8PM brief — macro+crypto week-in-review, gate status, asset stack, watchlist
- 05-Passive-Income-God.md: One new automated income stream per run — setup phases, Lindy integration, stack rotation
- 06-Bot-Optimization-God.md: Zero-noise bot monitoring — alerts only on high-confluence breaches, Wyckoff alignment
- 07-Dashboard-Matrix-Engine-God.md: Matrix-styled HTML/JS/CSS generator enforcing full design system compliance
- 08-Self-Upgrading-Architect-God.md: Reviews upgrade log, implements top suggestion, compounds arsenal intelligence
- 09-Hard-Asset-Tracker-God.md: Full hard-asset fortress brief — BTC/Land/Houses/Art/Metals, macro correlation table
- 10-Mira-Voice-God.md: Phonetic command router — translates any spoken command to correct template + voice readback

god-templates.html: Matrix-styled Arsenal page with copy-to-clipboard, search, holo-glow cards, matrix-rain background
js/god-templates.js: IIFE engine — template loader, clipboard API, fallback execCommand, toast notifications, search filter
css/matrix.css: God Templates nav highlight, holo-pulse animation, .gt- selector rules
scripts/update_data.py: inject_god_templates_into_data() + standalone --load-god-templates flag

14 HTML pages: Added "GOD TEMPLATES" nav link after CHARTS on every page

commit.message:
Staging → Main (#193)

* feat: add authenticated E2E tests for full organizer-participant booking flow

Add Playwright auth setup with seed accounts and a full happy-path spec:
organizer creates/publishes event → participant books → self-checks-in →
organizer verifies check-in on dashboard. Also fix pre-existing failures
in auth, home, and navigation specs (strict mode violations, splash screen overlay).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: organizer participant management — manually add participants and set paid/reserved/pending status

Organizers can now add participants directly from the event dashboard,
either by searching existing users or entering a name+contact for walk-ins.
Includes inline status dropdown, RLS policy for organizer inserts, and
migration for new booking columns (added_by, manual_status, manual_name, manual_contact).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: organizer auth check — compare against organizer_profiles.id, not users.id

events.organizer_id references organizer_profiles.id, so the auth check
was always failing. Now looks up the organizer profile by user_id first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: status dropdown clipped by table overflow-hidden

Removed overflow-hidden from desktop table container so the manual
status dropdown can render outside the table bounds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: disable participant management for completed events, fix loader timing

- Hide "Add Participant" button when event is completed
- Show static status badge instead of dropdown for completed events
- Fix loader disappearing before data refreshes by not clearing loading
  state (component re-renders with new data naturally)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: defer Leaflet loading and dynamic import waitlist modal

Gate StravaShowcaseRouteMap with IntersectionObserver so the Leaflet
chunk (~44 KiB) only loads when scrolled into view instead of on
initial page hydration. Dynamic import OrganizerWaitlistModal since
it's a modal behind a 3s delay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: enforce max capacity when organizer adds participants

Count active bookings + non-cancelled companions before allowing
a new participant to be added.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: status dropdown stuck in loading — use useTransition to track refresh

Local state persisted across re-renders since router.refresh() doesn't
unmount the component. Now uses useTransition so isPending tracks when
the Server Component finishes re-rendering with new data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: disable add participant button at capacity, use optimistic status updates

- Wire isFull prop through ParticipantsSection to disable button when at max capacity
- Replace useTransition with optimistic updates for status dropdown — each dropdown
  updates independently without blocking others
- Show "Event is at full capacity" text when button is disabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: convert waitlist modal to inline section (#181)

* refactor: convert organizer waitlist modal to inline homepage section

Replace the auto-popup OrganizerWaitlistModal with an inline
OrganizerWaitlistSection that renders naturally in the homepage scroll
flow. Removes the distracting 3-second delay popup behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: inject organizer_waitlist section when missing from CMS data

The CMS database has pre-configured sections that don't include the
new organizer_waitlist key, so it never rendered. Now the section is
auto-injected after pioneer_counter if absent from the CMS list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: place organizer waitlist section above pioneer counter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show system badges in GamificationSection instead of event badges (#182)

The query used events!inner(title) which required event badge rows to
exist. When seeded events are removed, the section disappears entirely.
Switch to type='system' badges so the section always renders.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove stale OrganizerWaitlistModal dynamic import (#184)

The modal file was deleted in #181 but a dynamic import remained,
causing an unused-variable lint error on staging CI.

feat: ImageUploaderコンポーネント追加＋武器/キャラシートに画像アップロード統合

- ImageUploaderコンポーネント＋CSS＋storage.jsを追加

shipping field corrections across multiple pages.

commit.message:
Also strip strand suffix in normalized epistasis ID matching

The lookup DB stores keys without strand (GENE:1:100:A:G) while the
input data includes strand suffixes (GENE:1:100:A:G:P). Normalize to
_:CHROM:POS:REF:ALT so both formats match.

commit.message:
fix: state sharing (/submit) never fired due to removed globalUrl field

pollOnce() checked _gs.globalUrl from getUiSettings(), but globalUrl was
removed from that function in a previous commit. _gs.globalUrl was always ""
so the condition was always false and /submit was never called.

Fix: use effectiveGlobalUrl(settings) which falls back to window.location.origin
(settings is already available as a parameter in pollOnce).
Also add console.error in submitSnapshotToGlobal on HTTP error or exception.

relative to process.cwd() which is already packages/cli/, doubling the path.

Fix: add driver pod fallback for stuck CRD state

The Apache Spark Kubernetes Operator may stall and not update CRD state
from RunningHealthy to Succeeded after driver pod completes. Add a fallback
that checks driver pod phase/exit_code directly alongside CRD status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

rpc: eth_getBlockReceipts(): fix OOM - bound goroutines and concurrency (#19710)

close #19720 

Fix goroutine leak in GetReceipts loop: each tx spawned a goroutine
waiting on ctx.Done() to cancel the EVM, but ctx was shared across the
entire block execution, so all N goroutines stayed alive until
GetReceipts returned. Under concurrent requests for different blocks
this caused goroutines and EVMs to accumulate in memory, triggering OOM.
Fixed by adding a txDone channel closed immediately after
ApplyTransactionWithEVM - the goroutine now exits as soon as its tx
completes, keeping at most 1 goroutine alive at a time.

Add execSem semaphore (capacity max(1, GOMAXPROCS/2), env
R_EXEC_CONCURRENCY) to limit the number of blocks executing concurrently
in GetReceipts. Each parallel block execution holds its own
IntraBlockState which can be hundreds of MB for busy mainnet blocks;
without a bound, many concurrent requests for different blocks exhaust
memory. Requests already served from the LRU cache bypass the semaphore
entirely.

./run_perf_tests.py .... _eth_get_block_receipts_21M_20K.tar -t
500:10,5000:1

max=10.597s]

commit.message:
Add PDF viewer, docx/GDoc paste import, and zen mode (#159)

## Summary

- **PDF Viewer**: Upload and view PDFs in editor panes via Supabase
Storage. Lazy-loaded `PdfPane` component using `react-pdf`, backend
upload/signed-URL endpoints, Yjs `pane-meta` map for collaborative type
sync, upload button in `TextHeader`, PDF icon indicator in `SectionList`
- **Paste Import**: `PasteCleanerExtension` intercepts pasted HTML via
ProseMirror `transformPastedHTML` to clean Google Docs (strip wrapper
bolds, convert inline styles, unwrap redirect URLs) and Word HTML (strip
Mso classes, conditional comments, style blocks). `.docx` files
supported via drag-drop and paste using `mammoth` (lazy-loaded). All
output sanitized with DOMPurify
- **Zen Mode**: Hide all chrome (toolbars, sidebars, headers, dividers,
annotation panels) leaving only editor content and arrow overlay
visible. Enter via ActionConsole "zen" button, exit with double-Escape
(within 500ms)

## Test plan

- [x] Build passes with zero errors
- [x] 2040 unit tests pass (168 files)
- [x] 32 e2e tests pass (arrow-drawing tests are pre-existing flakes on
main)
- [x] Lint clean, no errors
- [ ] Verify zen mode enter/exit via ActionConsole button +
double-Escape
- [ ] Verify paste from Google Docs strips wrapper bold and converts
styles
- [ ] Verify .docx drag-drop converts to clean HTML
- [ ] Verify PDF upload, view, scroll, and remove

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
